useful_outcomes.rationale: Directly compares character, k-mer (overlapping/non-overlapping), and BPE tokenization across 44 genomic fine-tuning tasks — the central claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released analysis pipeline lets practitioners pick tokenizers per task using the published task suite.

plausible.rationale: Includes a Mamba SSM head-to-head BPE-vs-character ablation, lending mechanistic credibility to claims.

scalable.rationale: 44-task evaluation shows the methodology generalizes across genomic settings.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides 44 fine-tuning tasks plus splice/promoter probes as a directly borrowable validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: METAGENE-1 explicitly tailors a BPE tokenizer for metagenomic DNA/RNA over 1.5T base pairs, directly testing a tokenization scheme.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Paper reports tokenizer design choices and pretraining loss curves; model release will allow reuse of the tokenizer.

plausible.rationale: 7B-parameter autoregressive transformer trained on wastewater metagenomes provides credible large-scale evidence.

scalable.rationale: Tokenizer scales to 1.5 trillion bp corpus with explicit BPE design for genomic sequences.

how_to_validate.rationale: Pandemic-monitoring downstream tasks (pathogen detection) provide a borrowable tokenizer-evaluation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Comprehensive survey explicitly assesses tokenization strategies (k-mer, BPE, character) across genome language models.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a taxonomy and reference table that researchers can immediately use to pick a tokenizer.

plausible.rationale: Synthesizes existing literature; conclusions are descriptive rather than empirical.

scalable.rationale: Survey scope spans architectures and species but does not run new experiments.

how_to_validate.rationale: Catalogues evaluation paradigms (zero-shot/few-shot/fine-tune) that frame tokenizer comparisons.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Proposes a novel sequence-encoding scheme for lncRNA classification, partially supporting the tokenization-matters claim on RNA.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained checkpoint is released but the contribution is mostly downstream classification rather than tokenizer design.

plausible.rationale: Cross-species RNAChallenge results lend credibility to the encoding-scheme contribution.

scalable.rationale: Trained on combined GENCODE/RefSeq/NONCODE sets; scale is moderate compared with DNA FMs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Coding/non-coding RNA classification benchmark is a useful but narrow tokenizer validation lens.

useful_outcomes.rationale: SWING introduces an interaction-vocabulary tokenization for peptide-protein contacts, only tangentially related to DNA/RNA tokenization claims.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code is released but vocabulary is amino-acid based, providing limited direct benefit for nucleotide tokenizer choice.

plausible.rationale: Demonstrated on MHC and PPI tasks but does not study DNA/RNA tokenization biases per se.

scalable.rationale: Sliding-window construction scales to long protein contexts but not nucleotide context length.

how_to_validate.rationale: MHC binding benchmarks are not transferable validation tasks for genomic tokenizer choice.

useful_outcomes.rationale: DNALongBench targets five long-range tasks up to 1Mb (3D folding, eQTL, enhancer-target), directly underwriting the long-context claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Releases benchmark plus baselines (HyenaDNA, Caduceus variants) that practitioners can drop into their pipeline.

plausible.rationale: Evaluates long-range vs short-context models head-to-head, lending empirical weight.

scalable.rationale: Tasks span 1-million-bp dependencies, the regime targeted by SSM/Hyena/Mamba architectures.

how_to_validate.rationale: Five canonical long-range tasks with public baselines provide a direct validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates selective state-space models (Mamba) outperform transformers at RNA-seq read coverage prediction, validating long-context SSM architectures.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code and trained models are public, enabling immediate reuse of striped Mamba architectures.

plausible.rationale: Striped Mamba+attention combination shows credible gains over Hyena and pure attention baselines.

scalable.rationale: Long-context Mamba inference scales better than dense attention for read-coverage prediction.

how_to_validate.rationale: GTEx eQTL benchmarks plus RNA-seq tasks form a transferable evaluation protocol for SSM claims.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Benchmarks five DNA foundation models including HyenaDNA and Caduceus-Ph, directly evaluating long-context architectures on genomic tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides reproducible task suite letting practitioners compare attention vs SSM/Hyena models immediately.

plausible.rationale: Empirically grounded comparison across diverse genomic tasks supports claim that architecture matters.

scalable.rationale: Models evaluated reach genome-scale embeddings but the benchmark itself uses moderate-length tasks.

how_to_validate.rationale: Releases a unified benchmark spanning sequence classification, expression, variant effects, and TADs.

useful_outcomes.rationale: PlantCAD2 is a long-context single-nucleotide-resolution DNA language model, demonstrating long-context architecture on plant genomes.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained checkpoint and benchmarks are released for plant-genomics users.

plausible.rationale: Outperforms 7B-parameter Evo2 on 10 of 12 zero-shot tasks, supporting long-context architecture claims.

scalable.rationale: Trained on 65 angiosperm genomes; 676M parameters with single-nt context unlocks distal regulatory modeling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cross-species zero-shot evaluations on chromatin and expression provide validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: RiNALMo uses masked-language-modeling self-supervision on 36M non-coding RNAs and learns implicit RNA structure — direct objective evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Largest open RNA LM (650M params); model and weights are released for downstream reuse.

plausible.rationale: Achieves SOTA on RNA structure prediction including unseen RNA families, lending strong credibility.

scalable.rationale: 650M parameters trained on ncRNA scale demonstrates objective-driven scaling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Secondary structure and family generalization tasks provide a clear borrowable validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: CellFM uses a modified RetNet self-supervised objective on 100M cells, illustrating alternative pretraining objectives in genomics-adjacent FMs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released model and benchmarks are usable for transcriptomic pretraining-objective comparisons.

plausible.rationale: Outperforms peer single-cell FMs on cell annotation/perturbation, lending empirical support.

scalable.rationale: 800M-parameter model on 100M-cell corpus shows objective-driven scaling at single-cell scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cell annotation, perturbation, and gene-function tasks provide validation protocols.

useful_outcomes.rationale: PepMLM fine-tunes ESM-2 with a masked-language-modeling objective conditioned on target sequences, exemplifying objective design but in protein space.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code/weights released enable direct reuse of the masked conditional objective.

plausible.rationale: Experimental binding validation supports the objective's effectiveness for sequence design.

scalable.rationale: Approach scales to arbitrary target proteins but does not address DNA/RNA pretraining.

how_to_validate.rationale: AlphaFold-based docking and binding/degradation assays provide validation procedure ideas.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Uses contrastive (CLIP-style) language modeling for peptide-target pairs, peripherally informing pretraining-objective design.

immediate_benefit.rationale: PepPrCLIP pipeline is released but is peptide- not nucleotide-focused.

plausible.rationale: Wet-lab validation of CLIP-pretraining for binders is credible but in protein space.

scalable.rationale: Latent perturbation generation scales to many peptide candidates, limited DNA/RNA relevance.

how_to_validate.rationale: Binder validation assays do not evaluate genomic pretraining objectives.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Pathology foundation models evaluated here use SSL on histology images, not DNA/RNA pretraining objectives.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Benchmarks are clinically valuable but not transferable to nucleotide pretraining objectives.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound but the modality is not nucleotide sequence.

scalable.rationale: Slide-level pretraining at clinical scale is impressive but orthogonal to genomic pretraining.

how_to_validate.rationale: Pathology endpoints do not validate genomic pretraining-objective claims.

useful_outcomes.rationale: CellWhisperer builds a contrastive multimodal embedding of transcriptomes and textual annotations from 1M RNA-seq profiles — direct shared-embedding evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Public CELLxGENE chat integration plus released embeddings are immediately usable.

plausible.rationale: Zero-shot cell-type prediction benchmarks confirm cross-modal retrieval works as claimed.

scalable.rationale: 1M-profile contrastive corpus shows the approach scales to large transcriptomic atlases.

how_to_validate.rationale: Zero-shot annotation prediction and embryonic-development meta-analysis provide validation protocols.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Uses protein language models to predict whole-genome pathogenic capacity; embeddings are sequence-only, not text-aligned.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Web server and code provide useful downstream tools but not multimodal text-sequence embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Empirically validated on novel taxa; the protein LM embedding usage is sound.

scalable.rationale: Whole-genome prediction at bacterial scale is reasonable but unimodal.

how_to_validate.rationale: Pathogenic-capacity benchmarks do not specifically validate text-grounded sequence retrieval.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reviews medical chatbots and LLM applications; does not propose a shared text-sequence embedding space.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful for risk discussion but not directly applicable to embedding-space construction.

plausible.rationale: Review-level claims, no empirical embedding work.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scalability of medical LLMs but no nucleotide modality.

how_to_validate.rationale: No validation protocol relevant to text-sequence embedding alignment.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Biomni is a general-purpose biomedical AI agent with retrieval-augmented planning and code execution across 25 domains — direct instruction-tuning evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-sourced agentic stack with tool catalog ready for immediate adoption.

plausible.rationale: Evaluated across diverse research tasks but agentic claims need broader external scrutiny.

scalable.rationale: Action-discovery agent harvests tools from tens of thousands of papers, demonstrating scalability.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multi-domain agentic benchmarks plus tool-use logs provide a validation protocol skeleton.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Systematically benchmarks zero/few-shot/fine-tuned LLMs on 12 BioNLP tasks, directly informing biomedical instruction-tuning practice.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open benchmark + recommendations let teams immediately calibrate their own instruction-tuned biomedical LLMs.

plausible.rationale: Compares GPT-4 and LLaMA representatives with traditional fine-tuning, lending strong empirical credibility.

scalable.rationale: Twelve benchmarks cover six biomedical NLP applications; modest scale of model coverage.

how_to_validate.rationale: Twelve-benchmark suite plus hallucination/cost analyses gives a directly borrowable evaluation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: GeneAgent is an instruction-tuned LLM agent that self-verifies gene-set analyses against biological databases — direct instruction-tuning evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code released; agent runs on existing LLMs and biological DBs, ready for biomedical use.

plausible.rationale: Outperforms GPT-4 on 1,106 gene sets, with expert review on novel sets confirming benefit.

scalable.rationale: Single-domain (gene-set analysis) limits scalability claims somewhat.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides 1,106-gene-set benchmark plus expert-review protocol for validation.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Builds an autonomous oncology AI agent on GPT-4 with multimodal precision tools and tool-use instruction tuning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Demonstrated 87.2% decision accuracy with reusable tool-orchestration design.

plausible.rationale: Validated on 20 realistic multimodal patient cases versus GPT-4 baseline.

scalable.rationale: Tool-use design is extendable but pilot-scale evaluation limits scalability claims.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides multimodal patient-case benchmark and tool-citation accuracy as validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: DeepRare is an LLM-driven agent integrating 40+ tools for rare-disease diagnosis, demonstrating instruction-tuned tool use grounded in HPO terms and genetic data.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Agent and reasoning traces released; plugs into existing clinical workflows.

plausible.rationale: Evaluated on 9 datasets across continents covering 14 specialties with 95.4% expert-validated reasoning.

scalable.rationale: Performance scales across heterogeneous clinical inputs but is rare-disease-specific.

how_to_validate.rationale: Recall@1 metrics on 2,919 diseases plus expert-reviewed reasoning chains form a validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Surveys 100+ biological AI agents, including instruction-tuned tool-use agents — supports claim descriptively.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a 5D taxonomy that teams can use to position their own instruction-tuned systems.

plausible.rationale: Synthesis is descriptive rather than experimental.

scalable.rationale: Covers a broad cross-section of agent designs across biomedical subfields.

how_to_validate.rationale: Identifies open evaluation challenges but supplies no new benchmark.

useful_outcomes.rationale: CodonTransformer conditions DNA-sequence generation on organism, amino-acid, and codon tokens — a clear text-conditioned sequence-generation system.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released open model and Colab interface let users generate host-specific DNA from natural-language-style organism specs immediately.

plausible.rationale: Trained on 1M DNA-protein pairs from 164 organisms; STREAM strategy is novel and credible.

scalable.rationale: Multispecies coverage and customizable model scale to broad host targets.

how_to_validate.rationale: Codon-distribution and cis-regulatory-element metrics provide a clear validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evo 2 predicts functional impact of genetic variants — including BRCA1 — without task-specific fine-tuning, central to the variant-effect claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained 7B/40B Evo 2 weights and inference code are released for immediate variant scoring.

plausible.rationale: 9.3T-bp training and clinical BRCA1 evaluation provide strong empirical credibility.

scalable.rationale: 1M-token context with single-nt resolution scales variant prediction to genome scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: BRCA1 clinical-significance and noncoding pathogenicity benchmarks form validation protocols.

useful_outcomes.rationale: AlphaGenome predicts regulatory variant effects across 24 of 26 evaluations using a unified 1Mb-context DNA model — direct variant-effect evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Public variant-effect prediction tools released alongside the paper.

plausible.rationale: Matches/exceeds best external models on most evaluations, with TAL1 mechanistic case study.

scalable.rationale: 1Mb input context across multiple modalities scales variant scoring to whole-locus regulatory effects.

how_to_validate.rationale: Twenty-six benchmark evaluations plus TAL1 clinical recapitulation provide a validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: popEVE provides proteome-wide calibrated variant deleteriousness scores, including 442 disease genes and 123 novel candidates — strong variant-effect evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Method publication usable for clinical genomics with code release indicated.

plausible.rationale: Combines evolutionary and population data in a deep generative model with rigorous benchmarking.

scalable.rationale: Scales across the full human proteome though limited to missense variants.

how_to_validate.rationale: Severe developmental disorder cohort and singleton-trio comparisons form validation protocols.

useful_outcomes.rationale: A Nature foundation model of transcription across human cell types delivers cell-type-specific regulatory annotations — direct regulatory-annotation evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Catalogues of gene regulation and TF interactions are released for downstream regulatory annotation.

plausible.rationale: Generalizable, accurate transcription model with cell-type specificity supported by Nature-level review.

scalable.rationale: Foundation model spans many human cell types, scaling regulatory annotation broadly.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides regulation catalogues and TF-interaction outputs as validation lenses.

useful_outcomes.rationale: TRAPT predicts transcriptional regulators by integrating cis-regulatory elements and binding-site epigenomics — direct regulatory-annotation evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open framework usable on new datasets to identify TRs in disease and cell-fate contexts.

plausible.rationale: Validated across 570 TR-related datasets versus SOTA baselines.

scalable.rationale: Multi-omics integration scales to new tissues but not to all regulatory element types.

how_to_validate.rationale: 570-dataset benchmark and disease/variant case studies serve as validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: PRINT and seq2PRINT infer transcription factor and nucleosome footprints from chromatin accessibility, annotating CREs at multiple scales — direct regulatory annotation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Public seq2PRINT framework can be applied to bulk and single-cell ATAC-seq data.

plausible.rationale: Nature paper with single-cell hematopoiesis case studies and aging perturbations.

scalable.rationale: Scales across the genome at multiple protein-size scales.

how_to_validate.rationale: Hematopoiesis and aging case studies plus footprint-recovery validation protocols.

useful_outcomes.rationale: ChromBERT learns interpretable transcriptional regulatory network representations from ~1,000 TRs' ChIP-seq — direct regulatory-element annotation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Foundation model with prompt-based fine-tuning is released for context-specific TRN inference.

plausible.rationale: Outperforms existing methods on cistrome imputation in a Cell Genomics paper.

scalable.rationale: Pretraining over ~1,000 TRs scales to wide regulatory coverage.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cistrome imputation and downstream cell-type-specific tasks provide validation lens.

useful_outcomes.rationale: C2S scales 27B LLMs on cell sentences plus literature/metadata to nominate silmitasertib as a context-selective immune drug — direct sequence+literature drug-discovery evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pre-trained 27B model and dual-context virtual-screen recipe are released for adoption.

plausible.rationale: Experimentally validated silmitasertib hit in human cells lends credibility to the LLM workflow.

scalable.rationale: 27B parameters trained on >1B tokens of transcriptomic+text data scales single-cell drug discovery.

how_to_validate.rationale: Perturbation-prediction benchmarks and the validated silmitasertib case form a validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: MMELON integrates graph, image and text foundation-model views for molecule-target and property prediction — directly multimodal drug discovery.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released multi-view framework usable for ADME and GPCR screens.

plausible.rationale: Validated across 120+ tasks including a GPCR virtual screen with structure-based confirmation.

scalable.rationale: Multi-view late-fusion is extendable but pretraining cost is non-trivial.

how_to_validate.rationale: 120+-task suite plus GPCR Alzheimer's-related virtual screen offers validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Predicts small-molecule-RNA interactions without tertiary structures, enabling RNA-targeted drug discovery from sequence alone.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Sequence-only RNA-ligand prediction tool ready for immediate use in RNA-therapeutic pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Nature Biotechnology venue and emerging-RNA-drug context lend credibility.

scalable.rationale: Sequence-based design scales to RNAs lacking solved structures.

how_to_validate.rationale: RNA-ligand benchmarks form a borrowable validation protocol.

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reviews AI in drug discovery (ML, DL, NLP) supporting the claim that LLMs accelerate drug R&D pipelines.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a roadmap of opportunities and challenges usable for strategic planning.

plausible.rationale: Descriptive synthesis without new experimental data.

scalable.rationale: Covers a broad range of stages from target ID to clinical trials.

how_to_validate.rationale: No new benchmark; outlines validation directions only.
